qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via JWT TokenIssuer template injection in metadata endpoint resolution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Does not match the advisory root cause: this finding targets unverified JWT issuer data reaching OAuth metadata/JWKS URL fetches, while the advisory targets case-sensitive HTTP host matching that can select an unintended rule.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause: case-sensitive host matching in Heimdall rule selection leading to policy bypass when a permissive default rule is configured.
evidence: The advisory describes hostnames being matched case-sensitively even though HTTP hostnames are case-insensitive, causing a differently cased Host header to miss an intended rule.

The ssrf-jwt-tokenissuer finding describes a JWT TokenIssuer metadata endpoint SSRF path through jwt_authenticator.go and oauth2 metadata fetching, not host rule case normalization or default-rule selection.
